name: Kythoni Peltes (Quince Jelly)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash quinces well to remove the fuzz. Peel and core.
2. Slice quinces into a preserving pan and add 2 cups water. Leave aside.
3. Place peels and cores into a pan with remaining water and boil for 30 minutes. Strain and make liquid up to 2 cups with water. Add liquid from peels to sliced quinces in the pan.
4. Bring to a boil and simmer gently for 1 hour until quince flesh is very tender.
5. Scald a large piece of cheese cloth or doubled butter muslin, wring out, and drape over a deep bowl. Pour quince and liquid into clean cloth and gather up ends. Tie with string and suspend over the bowl.
6. Secure to a fixed object so that juice can drip slowly into the bowl. Leave for 24 hours. Do not squeeze the bag to hasten dripping as this will make jelly cloudy.
7. Measure juice into a clean preserving pan. For each cup of juice, add 1 cup sugar. Stir over heat until sugar dissolves, then add lemon juice and washed geranium leaves and bring to a boil.
8. Boil rapidly for 25 minutes, skimming frequently. Test a teaspoonful on a cold saucer. Leave to cool.
9. Run finger across jelly in the saucer - setting point is reached when the surface wrinkles. Remove pan from heat while jelly is being tested as you would overcook the jelly.
10. Remove leaves and ladle hot jelly into hot sterilized jars. Seal when cold.
